content: Tribulus is derived from ancient Chinese, Indian, and Greek herbs and has been used for centuries to balance hormones, including increasing DHT and DHEA.
Protodioscin, an active component in Tribulus, helps increase bioavailable free testosterone levels by separating molecules that allow testosterone to be usable.
Maca root is a hormone modulator that works across all endocrine glands, balancing adrenal, thyroid, and testosterone functions, and modulating estrogen.
Dimethylated indole-3-carbinol (dim) is a concentrated form of estrogen modulators found in cruciferous vegetables, specifically targeting 16-hydroxyestradiol and supporting 2-hydroxyestradiol.
